item.title: Re: Copyright info
item.content:encoded:
For reference purposes, image license is as follows.

Source: Wikimedia Commons
Author: Maxime Raynal
License: CC BY 2.0

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can't wait till we find out who runs the lofi hip hop beats for studying streams

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
DJ Chaac felt real from the getgo. I just had to give this article the love it deserved.

+1.

item.title: Same thing here
item.content:encoded: https://youtu.be/gZR8ujT8l0A

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Same on all accounts; especially that first part about the dream logic fuckery. As someone who's listened to a rain ambient video at 2 AM, wondering how long it'll be before these tracks become relics of a time with rain… The description tripped me up and dragged me in.

I also did not ask to feel feelings for a Youtube music streamer. The voiceover on this was phenomenal. It sneaks up on you – the voice belongs to a streamer who just wants to make people happy with their music – and hits you with their grief. When it comes to controlling when water falls, DJ Chaac, at the very least, almost made me cry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am an ASMR enthusiast and I approve of this scip. (To be frank, I figured it was only a matter of time.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First sentence of the description made me upvote. The rest just confirmed that vote.

Very good work, Rimple.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sort of fitting, don't you think?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This skip makes me think of a post a while back that went something like…

Me, a standup comedian: And what of the rain? When she comes, she heals us, and is good. We love the rain.
Audience: We love the rain.

It inspires a weird sense of awe. I love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-3880 is a phenomenon whereby a small percentage of people indoors during rain storms will become convinced they are not hearing a storm, but instead are listening to the YouTube livestream ILLEST RAIN SOUNDS ∞ Hours No Looping - White Noise, Nature/Healing/Ambient, Meditation/Insomnia/Study ASMR [ORIGINAL], hosted by user DJ Chaac.

Sealed my upvote right fucking there. Dream logic fuckery of the highest order.

Then it got better. Then I almost cried in an Arby's.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Real life has got no repeats".

Identical twins are a thing, DJ Chaac.

In all seriousness, this gave me a real "American Gods" feeling. The character you build for DJ Chaac is very sympathetic, and I do like the glimpse into the divine politics you have provided in the final transcript. This is just an all around great interpretation of the prompt.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And your stream got taken down for some reason. Eerie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: From the perspective of this YouTuber, this comes of as 100% authentic to me; you really nailed the voice of the story, the character, and the subject matter. 10/10 would recommend.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First fan art of Chaac! To my knowledge, this is the first piece of fan art of the competition, and that makes my heart sing. Many thanks to WindyCat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like it, especially the fact that you took time to record those logs and all this stuff :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love DJChaac-tus's personality, and think that this article is great as is. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Poor Chaac. I feel for him. He just wanted to make nice rain for everybody. I hope life goes well for him. Also I'm in the screenshot wahey

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I appreciate the effort put into this, but… I dunno, maybe it's because I've never really participated in music streams or something, this just did absolutely nothing for me, I found it entirely uninteresting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Echoing MMG above me here. Fave entry for Day 3 for sure, and possibly for the contest as a whole. The care and love you put into this are really clear, and the whole thing is just brilliantly constructed. It's beautiful, it's immersive, and as someone who loves the sound of rain on a roof, it's a hearty +1 from me. I'd +2 if I could.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To be honest, if I had heard the idea described to me I wouldn't have been impressed, but it's clear a lot of care has been put into this, with the image, audio logs, transcript, and actual youtube page all coming together to make something quite simple unique and genuinely affecting.

Favourite entry of day 3.

item.title: Copyright info
item.content:encoded: Picture is a screenshot of a livestream I hosted - image used for the video is CC, from wikipedia Commons. All storm noise is from FreeSound, an open-source recordings library. I think that's it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked this. I love the rain, immensely. It makes me feel happy, I don't mind if it gets everywhere or if I walk through the door shivering and sopping wet, it just fills me with primal joy. Can't not be happy when it's raining outside. Also, white noise is my jam, so.

…Oh, and the writing and voice acting was good. So that too.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No relation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just odd. I am fond of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Poor DJ Chaac…that last log really got to me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've never made an SCP with audio before. I'd never even used audacity except to directly record my voice and then send that raw, no editing. Therefore it was very wise of me to decide I'd learn all that, PLUS learn how to stream on YouTube, in one day, while also attending lectures and doing an exam that counted for 20% of a module.

This is my third entry for the 72-hour Jam, and probably my favourite of the 3 - DJ Chaac has very quickly come to mean a lot to me. Fingers crossed that love comes across, even if half of what I'm actually saying doesn't. My other entries are SCP-3772 and SCP-3224, so go check those out.

Thanks to Stallmantic and my offsite friend Grimble for feedback in the early stages, when this was just a skeleton with audio. Thanks to LordStonefish for being the only person to read the first draft, and liking it so much they didn't notice I hadn't written any containment procedures.

DJ Rimp out.

PS: That stream is real. I'm playing chill music on it rn. Who knows what it'll be when you click.
